note: Paths inside a fixture are the capture's own. The conformance suite never edits a fixture on disk; it rebases the fields named in `rebase` onto a temp workspace (relative paths joined with path.join, so the suite is cross-platform).

fixtures.proves: cwd is workspaceFolders[0] = the workspace's own .devmate folder; the target path key is tool_input.filePath, never tool_input.path

fixtures.source: Envelope captured verbatim (same log, session 90d5b813); the agent log elides tool_input to "..." for edit tools, so tool_input is reconstructed from the replace_string_in_file input schema

fixtures.source: VS Code hooks reference + the run_in_terminal input schema
fixtures.proves: the shell tool is classified by what its command does, not by its name

fixtures.source: VS Code hooks reference; tool_input.agentName is [UNVERIFIED] — the agent log elides runSubagent tool_input to "...", so no capture can confirm it
fixtures.proves: HITL-1's first (best-effort) layer; the structural layer is SubagentStart, which reads the verified agent_type

fixtures.source: VS Code Copilot agent hook log (Output panel), 2026-07-13, session fd634936, model claude-sonnet-4.6, monoroot multi-root workspace
fixtures.proves: a subagent's result arrives as `tool_response`: a plain STRING holding the agent's final text (prose + an embedded JSON object), NOT a structured object and NOT a { content } wrapper. `tool_input` is elided to the literal "..." by the log, so tool_input.agentName remains unverifiable — the agent's identity must be read from the `agentName` field inside its own returned JSON (devmate's output contract), never from tool_input.

fixtures.source: VS Code Copilot agent hook log (Output panel), 2026-07-13, session fd634936, model claude-sonnet-4.6, monoroot multi-root workspace. Lifted verbatim from the ordered event stream in sessions/feature-lane-router.session.json (same capture) so the single event can be asserted on its own.
fixtures.proves: the ONLY identity a SubagentStart carries is `agent_type` + `agent_id` — and `agent_id` IS the parent link: it is exactly the `tool_use_id` of the `runSubagent` PreToolUse that spawned this subagent (`toolu_bdrk_01UqQ3JGUVF9NsqCmRoSWWkW`), minus the host's `__vscode-<n>` suffix. So a child CAN be tied to the tool call that made it. It cannot be tied to an EDIT: a PreToolUse payload carries no agent_id/agent_type at all (captured/pretooluse.read-file.json), so there is nothing on an edit event to join this link against — which is why gate-guard Rule 5 (per-edit persona attribution) was deleted rather than wired (#99).

fixtures.source: VS Code hooks reference: PostToolUse = PreToolUse fields + tool_response
fixtures.proves: memory collection and the action audit key on the real task, from the resolved root
